title: System intent satisfaction: improvement signals
scope: Governing intent: Convert repeated steering, proof confusion, handoff repair, context overload, or late failure into product improvement signals.
goal: Assess whether feedback and dogfooding findings have a disciplined route into product improvement.
non_goals: Do not create issues for this review unless a concrete repeated failure is found.
Do not treat every analysis opportunity as a queue item.

review_mode.review question: Does the package route repeated friction into issues, reviews, roadmap, docs, Memory, checks, or contracts without creating speculative queue churn?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: dogfooding feedback docs, planning review README, recent issue-driven workflow evidence

review_method.commands used: rg friction/improvement/signal/dogfooding/review across docs and planning review guidance; gh issue list --state open --limit 40 --json number,title,labels,updatedAt

findings.title: Satisfaction is high, with queue discipline as the important constraint
findings.summary: The repo has explicit dogfooding and review-promotion guidance, and recent work promoted concrete findings into issues before implementation. The system also warns not to convert concept opportunity into backlog by default.
findings.evidence: planning review README says friction-confirmed findings have higher trust, staged promotion is required, and weak/duplicate findings should be dismissed; dogfooding feedback says concept opportunity alone is not enough; #844-#846 were filed, implemented, validated, and closed.
findings.risk if unchanged: The improvement loop can still overproduce review artifacts or issues if agents promote every plausible idea rather than repeated or evidence-backed friction.
findings.suggested action: Keep current policy; only promote if future agents create speculative issue churn without friction evidence.
findings.promotion target: review-promotion lane if issue/review churn recurs
findings.promotion trigger: Multiple new review findings or issues lack friction, proof, or explicit maintainer override.

recommendation.defer: Watch issue/review churn during future optimization loops.
recommendation.dismiss: No new improvement-signal mechanism is needed now.

retention.proof surface: dogfooding feedback docs, planning review rules, issue closeout trail

drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
